sourceDesc.p: Derived from online catalogues of Hathi Trust, the Bodleian Library, the British Library, and the Library Hub

revisionDesc.change: Tagged in TEI and exposed on Digital Lacy website

body.p: This short bibliography lists in order of publication date most printed works attributed to Pascoe which are now available online. Some of these titles are effectively serials, reappearing annually with some modification but essentially the same content. The list does not include all such re-issues. Download links are included where possible. Where a title is not available online, I have indicated its shelf mark in the British Library, or a link to its entry in the Bodleian catalogue.
Please raise an issue on the Github repository if you spot any errors or omissions.

div.head: Published reference works and guides available online

bibl.title: The prohibitory law of Massachusetts versus the licensing law of England : with an account of the latter / by Charles E. Pascoe

A London directory for American travellers for 1874. Containing the fullest information, in the best form for reference, respecting all that is valuable in connection with a visit to London. With an appendix. By Charles E. Pascoe
bibl.publisher: Boston: Lee and Shepard; Lee, Shepard, and Dillingham, 1874

bibl.title: A practical handbook to the principal schools of England, based for the most part on the statutes, schemes, and regulations made under the Public Schools' Acts by the Public Schools Commissioners; ... A handy guide for the use of parents, guardians, and others interested in the education of boys.
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson Low, Marston, Searle & Rivington

bibl.title: The illustrated handbook to the supplementary art galleries of London : spring exhibitions, 1877

A practical handbook to the principal professions : compiled from authentic sources, and based on the most recent regulations concerning admission to the Navy, Army, and Civil Services (Home and Indian), the legal and medical professions, the professions of a civil engineer, architect, and artist, and the mercantile marine / by Charles Eyre Pascoe

listBibl.bibl: 1879Schools for girls and colleges for women : a handbook of female education chiefly designed for the use of persons of the upper middle class : together with some chapters on the higher employment of women London : Hardwicke and BogueschoolsForGirls_1879; CUP reprint

bibl.title: Dramatic notes : an illustrated handbook of the London theatres 1879
bibl.publisher: London: David Bogue: 3, St. Martin's Place, Trafalgar Square.

bibl.title: The dramatic list; a record of the principal performances of living actors and actresses of the British stage. With criticisms from contemporary journals. Comp. and ed. by Charles Eyre Pascoe
The dramatic list : a record of the performances of living actors and actresses of the British stage. Edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Second edition, revised and enlarged.
Everyday Life in our Public Schools. Sketched by head-scholars ... to which is added ... with a glossary of some words... Edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe. New and revised edition with 24 illustrations.
Where shall I educate my son? A manual for parents of moderate means / By Charles Eyre Pascoe
London of to-day : an illustrated handbook for the season
London of to-day : an illustrated handbook for the season.. Second Year of Publication, and Third Edition
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson, Low, Marston, Searle & Rivington, 1886

bibl.title: "The joyous neighbourhood of Covent Garden" : a literary souvenir of the Tavistock Hotel, done in celebration of its hundredth anniversary / by Charles Eyre Pascoe. The illus. contributed by John Jellicoe
London of to-day; an illustrated handbook for the season... The Fourth Annual Edition, completely revised, with numerous additional illustrations

listBibl.bibl: 1889London of to-day; an illustrated handbook for the season 1889... The Fifth Annual Edition, with numerous additional illustrations Boston: Roberts Brothers. londonToday_1889

bibl.title: Brighton of to-day : an illustrated book for all seasons, but more particularly those of 1889 / by Charles Eyre Pascoe

Everyday life in our public schools : sketched by head-scholars of Eton, Winchester, Westminster, Shrewsbury, Harrow, Rugby, Charterhouse. To which is added a brief notice of St. Paul's and Merchant Taylors' schools, and Christ's hospital ; with a glossary of some words in common use in those schools / edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe; with 24 illustrations
bibl.publisher: London: Griffith and Farran ; E.P. Dutton, [188-?]

bibl.title: London of to-day. An illustrated handbook for the season 1890... The sixth Annual Edition, revised and in part rewritten, and comprising numerous additional illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers. 1890

bibl.title: London of to-day an illustrated handbook for the season 1891... The Seventh Annual Edition, revised and in part rewritten, and comprising numerous additional illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers. 1891

bibl.title: Brighton; a pocket-book of local information for visitors. Illustrated by Holland Tringham and W. Luker, jun
The American roads through England: An Illustrated Pocket-book of Matter-of-fact Information; ... (Special Edition for use of visitors at the Adelphi hotel, Liverpool)
bibl.publisher: England: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: The American roads through England; a pocket-book of matter-of-fact information. Compiled for the use of American travellers
bibl.publisher: England: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: The roads to Paris, from London: A Pocket-book for those who are unfamiliar with them. Compiled by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by H. Tringham.
bibl.publisher: London: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: London of today: an illustrated handbook for the season, 1892... The Eighth Annual Edition, revised and in large part re-written, and comprising additional Illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers 1892

bibl.title: London of To-day. An illustrated handbook for the season, 1893... The Ninth Annual Edition, revised and in large part re-written, and comprising additional Illustrations.

listBibl.bibl: 1893An illustrated souvenir of Victoria House, the head-quarters of the Royal commission for Great Britain at the World's Columbian exposition, Chicago, 1893EnglandvictoriaHouse_1893`

bibl.title: London's world's fair for 1893 and Great Social, Politgical, and Moral Expososition. The Spirit of the Age at a glance. Explained by Charles Eyre Pascoe and Illustrated by Fred. Pegram
bibl.publisher: London: Samspon Low, Marston & Company Limited.

bibl.title: The London Season. The monthly edition of “London of To-Day.” NS. vol. 3. no. 25-27. May-July 1897.
London of To-day; an illustrated handbook for the season 1897. The Thirteenth Yearly Edition.Shilling souvenir edition, sixtieth year of the Queen's Reign.
bibl.publisher: London: Bemrose & sons Limited... and at the Office of "Lonmdon of To-Day," Hastings House, Norfolk Street, Strand, W.C.

bibl.title: London of today, handbook for the season 1902 / by Charles Eyre Pascoe [17th edition]
bibl.publisher: London : Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Adams, Kent & Co., Ltd. Paternoster Row, E.C.

bibl.title: The pageant & ceremony of the coronation of Their Majesties King Edward the Seventh and Queen Alexandra, by Charles Eyre Pascoe

No. 10, Downing Street, Whitehall: its history and associations ... The illustrations in colour, and other sketches, by Charles E. Flower. A Memory of Great NMames and an Inheritance of Great Examples.
York historic pageant : souvenir

The portrait book of our kings and queens, 1066-1911. Done in commemoration of the coronation of their Majesties King George V and Queen Mary. With supplementary notes on the ceremony. Edited by T. Leman Hare. The Historical and coronation Notes by Charles Eyre Pascoe
bibl.publisher: London and Edinburgh: T.C. & E.C. Jack

div.head: Pocket Guides and pamphlets not available online

bibl.title: Folkestone of to-day : an illustrated book for visitors; season 1889 / By Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by H. Tringham.
Eastbourne, Hastings, and St. Leonard's : a pocket-book of local information for visitors
Descriptive and pictorial account of Cromer, Sheringham, Mundesley, and the Broads
Descriptive and pictorial account of Scarborough, Whitby, Filey and Bridlington

div.bibl: 1898Holiday Leaflets Illustrated. Great Northern Series ... Written and arranged by C. E. Pascoe, etc. no. 1-5.London: Office of 'London of To-Day'BL 10347.e.48. (Contents: Three Days in the Dukeries; Cromer, Sheringham, Mondesley, and the Broads; A trip into Lincolnshire: Skegness, Mablethorpe, Sutton-on-Sea, and Woodhall Spa; An excursion into Yorkshire: Scarborough, Whitby, Filey, and Bridlington; Some Northern Health Resorts: Harrogate, Ilkley, and Ben Rhydding)

bibl.title: The Golf Grounds of the South-west: A Gossiping Summary of Their Various Attractions
bibl.publisher: Published at the office of "London of To-Day,"

bibl.title: Twelfth night: a plea for an old custom / (written by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by John Jellicoe.).

The Chambers of Commerce Year-Book. A directory of places in the United Kingdom where they are instituted, and a record of their aims and work. Supplemented by specially contributed articles and other information. Edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe. vol. 1. Jan. 1908-Jan. 1909.
English Notes for American Acceptance. An unconventional guide-book to the north of England, offered to Americans [illustrated]
bibl.publisher: York: Great Northern & North Eastern Railway Companies

bibl.title: Lovely May and leafy June : a seasonable suggestion: to many thousands of admirers of merrie England
bibl.publisher: (London and Tunbridge Wells) : Photochrome. Co. Ltd.

bibl.title: Dickens in Yorkshire : being notes of a journey to the delightful village of Dotheboys, near Greta Bridge.

div.head: Periodical contributions
div.p: Pascoe was a prolific contributor to magazines, both American and English. Many of these contributions (notably the two series contributed to the Leisure Hour) were subsequently recycled in a book or guide. Many titles mentioned elsewhere are yet to be traced. For those which have been found online, a link is provided below.

listBibl.bibl: “In Memoriam: Temple Bar.” Illus. New York: Appleton’s journal. vol 2 (1877) pp 75-81
“An English By-Lane”, New York: Appleton’s journal. Vol 1 (1876) pp 231-233
`Christ’s Hospital’ (“Great Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Mar 1, 1879): issue 1418 pp 135-140.
`The Charterhouse School’ (“Great Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Jul 26, 1879): Issue 1439, pp 471-475.
`St Paul’s School’ (“Great Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Nov 29, 1879): Issue 1457, pp 763-766.
`Merchant Taylors’ School (“Great Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour(Dec 13, 1879): Issue 1459, pp 796-800.
`"First Nights" at London Theatres' The American (27 November 1880), pp 103-104
`Story of the English magazines’ Atlantic Monthly, (Sept 1884), pp 364-374. [1884_englishMagazines.pdf]
1884 `Buying a house’ The Leisure hour, Jan. 1877-Oct. 1903; London (May 1884): 294-298. [buyingHouse1884.pdf]
`A Ramble round Windsor’ The Leisure hour (Apr 1887): pp 251-260.
`The Queen’s Homes. Introductory - the Queen's Household’ The Leisure hour (Jan 1888): pp. 16-21; [1888_queensHomes1.pdf
`The Queen’s Homes: Kensington Palace’ The Leisure hour (Feb 1888) pp 115-123;
`The Queen’s Homes: Frogmore House’. Illus Victor Prout. The Leisure Hour (Mar 1888) pp 167-174.
`The Queen’s Homes: Claremont’ The Leisure hour (Apr 1888: 237-241;
`The Queen’s Homes: Buckingham Palace’ The Leisure hour (May 1888) pp 317-324. ;
`Downing Street: the Official Residence of the First Lord of the Treasury. With Illustrations by Philip Norman. The Leisure hour, (Jun 1888) pp 374-384.
`The Queen’s Homes: Windsor Castle’The Leisure hour (Aug 1888) pp 515-524.
`The Queen’s Homes: Osborne’ The Leisure hour (Sep 1888) pp 598-604.
`The Queen’s Homes: Balmoral' The Leisure hour (Nov 1888) pp 753-759.

div.head: Newspaper correspondence
div.p: Pascoe was also an indefatigable writer of letters to the editor, frequently appearing as a harassed radical in The Daily News, which was the leading "Liberal" journal of his day.

listBibl.bibl: 1867 "The Reception of the Belgians." letter to the editor Royal Cornwall Gazette. (May 16, 1867) Issue 3331, p.6
1884 “American Payments to English Authors” letter in The Athenaeum. (Nov 8, 1884): p 592 Iss. 2976/
1886 "The Theatres" [quotes a letter from CEP] Daily News, (June 7, 1886) Iss. 12529
1889 “The Maybrick case” Daily News (Aug 13, 1889) Iss. 13526, p.2
1896 “A word for the skipper” Daily News (July 30, 1896) Issue 15706
1896 “A literary Londoner in Liverpool” letter to Liverpool Daily Post, 25 May 1896
1896 “Is it fact or flourish of phrases?” letter to Daily News, (Oct 15 1896) Issue 15772, p.3 [media/1896-10-15-DailyNews_p3_Letter.png]
1899 “The Transvaal Crisis” letters to Daily News Sep 30 1899 Issue 16698, p.6 ; Dec 30 1899, p.9; Jan 3, 1900, p.3

bibl.title: [Application to Royal Literary Fund] Charles Eyre Pascoe B 1842

listBibl.bibl: 1912Barking of Dogs and sudden Death [Report of Pascoe's death from the Sutton and Cheam Advertiser, 15 Nov 1912, page 5.] Transcription of the report
